title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Haemul Pajeon, the crispy Korean seafood pancake, at home! This easy recipe brings the flavors of Korea to your kitchen. Perfect for a delicious meal.
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step Instructions
Tips for the Crispiest Haemul Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: Crispy Haemul Pajeon, a delicious Korean seafood pancake, served with dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a homemade, crispy Haemul Pajeon, packed with fresh seafood and green onions, just like in Korea!

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, generously loaded with fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. Its crispy edges and chewy interior make it an irresistible appetizer or a light meal. This traditional dish is perfect for sharing and pairs wonderfully with a tangy soy-vinegar dipping sauce.

ingredients.notes: shrimp, squid, mussels, scallops, cut into bite-sized pieces
about 10-12 stalks, trimmed and cut into 3-4 inch lengths

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour, and cornstarch. Gradually add the cold water or anchovy broth, whisking until a smooth, lump-free batter forms. Beat in the egg until well combined. Set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Ensure your seafood is well drained and patted dry. Cut any larger pieces into bite-sized portions. Trim the green onions and cut them into 3-4 inch lengths. Thinly slice the onion and red chili if using.

instructions.title: Make the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, toasted sesame seeds, and red pepper flakes (if using). Stir well and set aside.

instructions.title: Assemble the Pancake
instructions.text: Heat a large non-stick pan or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat. Add about 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil. Pour a ladleful of batter into the hot pan to form a thin circle. Quickly arrange a generous handful of green onion pieces, sliced onion, and an even layer of mixed seafood on top of the wet batter.

instructions.title: Cook the First Side
instructions.text: Pour a little more batter over the toppings, just enough to bind them. Cook for 3-5 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, and the edges begin to set. Drizzle a little more oil around the edges to ensure crispiness.

instructions.title: Flip and Finish Cooking
instructions.text: Carefully flip the pancake using a large spatula. Cook for another 3-5 minutes on the second side until it's also golden brown and the seafood is cooked through. You can press down lightly with the spatula to ensure even cooking and crispiness.

instructions.title: Repeat and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the cooked pancake from the pan and place it on a cutting board. Cut into wedges. Repeat with the remaining batter, seafood, and vegetables, adding more oil to the pan as needed. Serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

tips: Use very cold water or even sparkling water for the batter to achieve a crispier pancake. This helps prevent gluten development.
For extra flavor, use homemade anchovy broth instead of plain water in the batter. This adds a subtle umami depth.
Do not overcrowd the pan. Cook one pancake at a time to ensure even cooking and a perfectly crispy texture. Serve immediately for best results.
article:
Savoring the Ocean's Bounty: Your Ultimate Guide to Haemul Pajeon, the Beloved Korean Seafood Pancake

Imagine a culinary masterpiece that perfectly marries the freshness of the sea with the comforting crispness of a savory pancake. That, my friends, is Haemul Pajeon – the iconic Korean seafood pancake that has captured hearts and palates worldwide. More than just a dish, Haemul Pajeon is a celebration of texture, flavor, and communal dining, making it a staple in Korean homes, restaurants, and street food stalls. Whether you're a seasoned Korean food enthusiast or new to the vibrant flavors of the peninsula, understanding and mastering Haemul Pajeon is a delightful journey. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know about this delectable dish, from its origins to the secrets behind its perfect crisp.

## What is Haemul Pajeon?

At its core, Haemul Pajeon (해물파전) translates directly to "seafood green onion pancake." It's a type of pajeon, a broader category of Korean savory pancakes, distinguished by its generous inclusion of various fresh seafood and an abundance of green onions (scallions). Unlike some other pancakes that might be thick and doughy, a good Haemul Pajeon boasts a delightful contrast: a tender, flavorful interior packed with succulent seafood and aromatic green onions, encased in an irresistibly crispy, golden-brown crust.

The beauty of Haemul Pajeon lies in its simplicity and its ability to highlight the natural sweetness of the ocean's bounty. The batter, typically a light mix of flour, water, and sometimes egg, acts as a delicate binder, allowing the star ingredients – shrimp, squid, mussels, and often oysters or clams – to shine. Cooked on a hot griddle with a generous amount of oil, the pancake develops a unique texture that is both satisfyingly chewy and wonderfully crisp. It’s often enjoyed as an appetizer, a side dish (banchan), or a perfect accompaniment to a glass of makgeolli (Korean rice wine) on a rainy day.

## Ingredients for Haemul Pajeon

Creating an authentic Haemul Pajeon starts with selecting the right ingredients. Quality and freshness are key, especially when it comes to the seafood.

For the Batter:
*   1 cup all-purpose flour (or a mix of all-purpose and glutinous rice flour for extra chewiness)
*   1/2 cup cold water (or anchovy broth for deeper flavor)
*   1 large egg
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   Pinch of black pepper

For the Filling:
*   1 cup mixed fresh seafood (shrimp, squid, mussels, oysters, clams – cleaned and chopped into bite-sized pieces)
*   1 bunch green onions (about 6-8 stalks), cut into 2-3 inch lengths, with some thinly sliced for garnish
*   1/2 small onion, thinly sliced (optional)
*   1/4 cup other vegetables (e.g., thinly sliced carrot, red chili for color and a touch of heat – optional)
*   2-3 tablespoons vegetable oil (or neutral oil) for cooking

For the Dipping Sauce (Yangnyeom Ganjang):
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon water
*   1 teaspoon rice vinegar
*   1/2 teaspoon sugar (or mirin)
*   1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes – optional)
*   1/2 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1/2 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   1 teaspoon finely chopped green onion

## How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Haemul Pajeon is surprisingly straightforward, yielding impressive results. Follow these steps for a perfect pancake every time.

1.  **Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables:**
    *   Clean all seafood thoroughly. For shrimp, peel and devein. For squid, clean and score the body, then slice into rings or strips. Mussels and clams should be scrubbed and de-bearded. Chop any larger pieces into bite-sized portions. Pat all seafood dry with paper towels to prevent a watery batter.
    *   Wash and trim the green onions. Cut the white and light green parts into 2-3 inch lengths. Thinly slice a small amount of the dark green parts for the dipping sauce and garnish.
    *   If using, thinly slice the onion, carrot, and chili.

2.  **Make the Batter:**
    *   In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, salt, and pepper.
    *   Add the cold water and egg. Whisk until just combined. A few lumps are okay; do not overmix, as this can lead to a tough pancake. The batter should be slightly thicker than crepe batter, able to coat the back of a spoon.

3.  **Assemble the Pancake Mix:**
    *   Add the prepared seafood, green onion lengths, and any optional vegetables to the batter. Gently fold them in until evenly coated.

4.  **Cook the Haemul Pajeon:**
    *   Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil, ensuring the pan is well-coated. The oil needs to be hot enough to sizzle immediately when the batter is added.
    *   Pour about 1/2 to 1 cup of the batter mixture into the hot pan, spreading it evenly to form a round pancake about 6-8 inches in diameter. You might need to use a spatula to arrange the green onions and seafood evenly across the surface.
    *   Cook for 4-5 minutes on the first side, or until the edges are golden brown and crispy, and the bottom is set.
    *   Carefully flip the pancake using a wide spatula. Add a little more oil around the edges if needed to encourage crispiness.
    *   Cook for another 3-4 minutes on the second side, until golden brown and cooked through. The seafood should be opaque and tender.

5.  **Serve:**
    *   Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board and slice into wedges.
    *   Repeat with the remaining batter, adding more oil to the pan as needed.
    *   While the pancakes are cooking, prepare the dipping sauce by combining all sauce ingredients in a small bowl.

## Tips for the Crispiest Haemul Pajeon

Achieving that perfect crisp exterior while maintaining a tender interior is the hallmark of an excellent Haemul Pajeon. Here are some pro tips:

*   **Use Cold Water:** Incorporating ice-cold water or even a few ice cubes into your batter helps prevent gluten development, resulting in a lighter, crisper texture.
*   **Don't Overmix the Batter:** Overmixing develops gluten, making the pancake tough. Mix just until the ingredients are combined.
*   **Hot Pan, Generous Oil:** Ensure your pan is hot before adding the batter, and don't skimp on the oil. The oil helps create that beautiful golden-brown crust. You can even drizzle a little extra around the edges while it's cooking.
*   **Thin Batter Layer:** While tempting to make a thick pancake, a thinner batter layer allows for more surface area to crisp up.
*   **Pat Seafood Dry:** Excess moisture from the seafood can make the pancake soggy. Thoroughly pat your seafood dry before adding it to the batter.
*   **Cook in Batches:** Don't overcrowd the pan. Cooking one or two pancakes at a time ensures consistent heat and crispiness.

## Serving Suggestions & Dipping Sauce

Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed immediately after cooking, while it's still hot and crispy. It’s often sliced into wedges or squares for easy sharing.

The accompanying dipping sauce (yangnyeom ganjang) is essential. Its tangy, savory, and slightly spicy notes perfectly complement the rich flavors of the pancake. Feel free to adjust the ingredients to your taste – add more chili for heat, or a touch of honey for sweetness.

In Korea, Haemul Pajeon is frequently paired with makgeolli, a milky, slightly sweet rice wine. The refreshing effervescence of makgeolli cuts through the richness of the pancake beautifully. It also makes a fantastic appetizer for any Korean meal, alongside other banchan like kimchi, or as a hearty snack.

## Haemul Pajeon Variations & FAQs

While the classic Haemul Pajeon is a delight, there are many ways to customize your pancake experience.

*   **Kimchi Pajeon:** For a spicy kick, add chopped kimchi to your batter. The fermented cabbage adds a wonderful tang and heat.
*   **Meat Pajeon (Gogi Pajeon):** Replace seafood with thinly sliced beef (bulgogi style) or pork for a different savory profile.
*   **Vegetable Pajeon:** Omit the seafood entirely and load up on various vegetables like mushrooms, bell peppers, zucchini, or carrots for a delicious vegetarian option.

**FAQs:**

*   **Can I make Haemul Pajeon gluten-free?** Yes, you can substitute the all-purpose flour with a gluten-free flour blend, often with good results.
*   **What's the best seafood to use?** A mix is best! Shrimp and squid are staples. Mussels, oysters, and even bay scallops are excellent additions.
*   **Can I prepare the batter ahead of time?** It's best to mix the batter and seafood just before cooking to prevent the seafood from releasing too much water and the batter from becoming soggy.
*   **How do I reheat leftover Haemul Pajeon?** The best way is to reheat it in a dry skillet over medium heat or in a toaster oven/air fryer until crispy again. Microwaving will make it soft.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just a dish; it's an experience. It's the perfect blend of simple ingredients transformed into something extraordinary, offering a symphony of textures and flavors that embodies the heart of Korean comfort food. So, gather your ingredients, fire up your pan, and get ready to create a truly unforgettable Korean seafood pancake that will transport your taste buds straight to the bustling streets of Seoul. Enjoy the culinary adventure!
